title: JSON Data Processor - Final Touch

content.problemStatement:
Great! You Have Successfully Built a **JSON Data Processor** Using C++ 🎉

Here are some improvements you can try:


**Add Student Feature** – Allow users to add new student records and save them to the JSON file.
**Add Data Backup** – Before deleting or modifying records, create a backup of the original `students.json`.
**Sorting Feature** – Let users sort student records by fields like name, CGPA, or age using custom sorting logic.
**Update Student Details** – Implement functionality to edit or update student details by ID.
**Partial Search by Name** – Enhance the search to allow partial string matching for names (e.g., "Amit" matches "Amit Sharma").
**Final Updates:**
Make sure all **loading, searching, filtering, and deleting** features perform accurately.
Handle edge cases like **non-numeric input, empty files, or missing fields** gracefully.
Improve user experience with **clear input messages and structured output display**.
Try implementing these enhancements to take your **JSON Data Processor** project to the next level! 🚀
content.inputFormat: First line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains an operation followed by its parameters separated by spaces. Supported operations are: add id name CGPA age, delete id, update id name CGPA age, search name, sort field
content.outputFormat: For each test case print a single JSON object on its own line representing the result of the operation. The formats are: add / update → {"id": id, "name": "name", "CGPA": cgpa, "age": age}; delete → {"id": id}; search → {"name": "name"}; sort → {"sorted_by": "field"}. All keys and string values are quoted exactly as shown, numbers are not quoted, and a single space follows each comma.

sampleTestCases.explanation: First test case adds a student, second deletes the same id, third updates the record. Each line prints the JSON representation defined for the operation.
The search operation echoes the searched name, and the sort operation echoes the field used for sorting.
Demonstrates handling of maximum allowed numeric values.

hidden.label: uniform case 2
hidden.input:
4
add 1 A 2.0 20
delete 1
add 2 B 3.0 21
delete 2

hidden.label: stress case
hidden.input:
5
add 10 Alice 3.2 19
search Alice
update 10 Alice 3.5 20
sort age
delete 10

hidden.label: stress case 2
hidden.input:
5
add 1 A 1 10
add 2 B 2 20
add 3 C 3 30
add 4 D 4 40
add 5 E 5 50

hidden.label: stress case 3
hidden.input:
4
delete 2
search B
delete 3
search C

hidden.label: stress case 4
hidden.input:
6
add 100 X 1.1 11
update 100 X 2.2 12
search X
sort CGPA
delete 100
search X

solution.approach: Read the integer T. Loop T times, read a non‑empty line, split it with StringTokenizer and identify the operation. For add and update we parse id, name, CGPA and age and directly print a JSON object with those fields. For delete we output only the id, for search we output the name, and for sort we output the field name under the key "sorted_by". The required spacing and quoting are produced by appending literals to a StringBuilder. The algorithm is O(T) time and O(1) auxiliary space (apart from the output buffer).
solution.timeComplexity: O(T) overall, O(1) per test case
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) extra space besides the output buffer

code.Java:
import java.io.*;import java.util.*;public class Main{public static void main(String[] args) throws Exception{BufferedReader br=new BufferedReader(new InputStreamReader(System.in));StringBuilder out=new StringBuilder();String line;line=br.readLine();while(line!=null&&line.trim().isEmpty()) line=br.readLine();int T=Integer.parseInt(line.trim());for(int t=0;t<T;t++){line=br.readLine();while(line!=null&&line.trim().isEmpty()) line=br.readLine();StringTokenizer st=new StringTokenizer(line);String op=st.nextToken();switch(op){case "add":case "update":{long id=Long.parseLong(st.nextToken());String name=st.nextToken();double cgpa=Double.parseDouble(st.nextToken());int age=Integer.parseInt(st.nextToken());out.append('{').append(""id": ").append(id).append(", "name": "").append(name).append('"').append(", "CGPA": ");if(cgpa==(long)cgpa){out.append((long)cgpa);}else{out.append(cgpa);}out.append(", "age": ").append(age).append('}').append('
');break;}case "delete":{long id=Long.parseLong(st.nextToken());out.append('{').append(""id": ").append(id).append('}').append('
');break;}case "search":{String name=st.nextToken();out.append('{').append(""name": "").append(name).append('"').append('}').append('
');break;}case "sort":{String field=st.nextToken();out.append('{').append(""sorted_by": "").append(field).append('"').append('}').append('
');break;}}}System.out.print(out.toString());}}
